Packaging display box check update 


The display unit is in good condition; each unit contains 18 pieces and features its respective "Best By" date and lot number printed directly on it. The flow wrap is also in good condition and appears securely sealed on both sides; it, too, bears the "Best By" date and lot number printed in the required location.
Yes Spencer is looking at it. Trying to make it so the rail doesn’t poke out at the beginning
Yes it was only a 10 minute pause

There was some miscommunication when an EE went to lunch. We were pausing for the pack end on the WDS, The EE on depositor 1 turned off sending the chocolate so in case the pause was longer we didn't over heat the chocolate. This was not communicated to the EE sending them to lunch so when we started back up the chocolate wasn't ready inside the depositor and it took a minute for the EE to notice. We have about 270 molds inside with no product which will be about 11-12 min of not running.

This is ML3   Broken/missing inspection tag

Three tempering cycles have been performed, yet the temperature remains high; adjustments are still being made to improve the situation, and the line remains down.

Raoul Lubain, Alan Greer,

What do you think we should do?
Gave instructions to reheat.
Thank you
Yes sir your welcome

Let’s manage the chocolate in the auger and make sure that doesn’t harden. 

Also for the hopper as well so we don’t get clogged nozzles starting up.
Will do.
St
Stopping to readjust again
Started reheat at 5:27 am
New temp looks good
Reheat completed; result: -0.2. The line is starting up.
There are more than 80 molds stuck with chocolate; they didn't start at the correct temperature, I can't deposit the chocolate because there are no clean molds.
Percentage of chocolate and rice within the range

When you say we didn’t start at the correct temperature. 

Can you expand on that for what you found?
Countdown

Rich Brierley,

Let’s get these molds to the mold washer (ensure we knock the chocolate off) and get them clean and back on the line.
What is the projected start time Rich Brierley?
Metal detector belt and c6 belt are running correctly

The flow of chocolate is halted. Upon exiting the tempering process, the temper reading is 1.1; it is measured again and reads 1.0. The flow of chocolate is then reactivated; a new temper reading is currently being taken, and an adjustment will be performed at 8:40 AM.
The weight of the bar of chocolate it’s on the target and dimensions also it’s on the target
An adjustment to the mixing water is performed at 8:38 AM; the transfer of chocolate continues. Temper readings will be taken every 15 minutes.
M230 belt is not working correctly
Maintenance is working on it
An adjustment is made to the mixing water at 9:13, to 83.9.

Good morning!
I was wondering when we can get an updated schedule for Friday? Maria Moron Quintero
Good morning! Sorry, I wasn’t aware of the changes for Friday. I’ll send the updated schedule in a moment. Thanks!
We have enough pretzels to run until roughly tomorrow at 11:00am. Martin Carlos, Sunny Jagtiani,Curt Willis
Martin Carlos I know you said this is delivering tomorrow.  Do we have a time
the pallets are on the  BOMs and consumed in the system.

Daniel Bringas and I talked 

S3 tomorrow we will run pretzels on 1st shift, we will end in back at 3:00 and begin clean with the goal to start s’mores at 7:00am on Saturday 

S2 will run pecans and we will do a level 4 at the end of the week. 

4 hours cleaning Friday night on 3rd shift and all three shifts on Saturday so that we can start on caramels on Monday morning

Good morning Team, FYI, for Monday's production, on S2 item 30521-Case8, we currently have 2,100 trays of item 40649-V2. We will transition to V4, for which we have 23,472 available. Miriah Manzanares Candon Watt Victor Romero Sr. Katya Farnsworth Justin Curt Willis Francisco Gudino Torres Ricardo Paula Pena Nidia Lopez Corea

Sounds good. I have talked with Wilson Amaris Cabrera and my line lead for ML3. We have started placing the WIP pallets aside for future rework. Once the 41125 boxes arrive we will put them on the line with the new work order

I will create the new WO

Yes it’s correct 👍
Good afternoon team, the boxes are designed to hold only 15 lbs. When packed with 20 lbs, they do not close properly and are not functional. Brandon Lance, Alan Todd, Hervin Herrera
Here it has 20 lbs
We will continue to run as we were until the correct box and item number can be determined.

We can only fit 15 pounds in those bulk boxes.
Of course!

Brandon Lance
Yes that’s the one I had a typo. We have plenty on site too.

This is the one that fits 20 lbs

from last year

Katya Farnsworth Nathan Topham please as soon as the EEs finish with lunch rotations make sure to send them over so we can complete the cleaning. Thank you.

This is the plan to continuing with the environmental clean on TG.

Rollers, belts, scrappers, catch trays, and all removable pieces will be steamed  and afterwards brought over to the washing area to be rinsed with PAA.
Frames will continue to be cleaned with the regular process and at the end rinsed as well with PAA.

The washing area will be followed with a heavy wash.

Charity Young Curt Willis Miriah Manzanares Justin Garner
When I tried to turn on Tunnel 2 this is what I got. Matt Hatch
Curt Willis

Good afternoon all, 
 
Chris Givens and I talked this morning about the parts needed for Cooling Tunnel 1 on S4.  This part will be delivering the week of 5/25.  We know from Jorge Carrillo ramp up that this will not work.  Curt Willis, FYI.
 
Truffle line will be down for 2 weeks (through 5/29) so we can use the parts needed and we just need to communicate clearly to Preston (L3) and Rose (L3) what as needed so we can plan for the installation of the new part once it arrives.

We are steaming the parts
Jorel prepare the PAA once everything is steamed we will take parts to the washing area

Waiting on confirmation from Charity Young to start using the PAA on the parts 
Curt Willis FYI
Do the PAA first then rinse very very very good and then QA will ATP and collect sample.

PAA was applied to parts and frame, we will let it dry for today, tomorrow we will clean with soap and rinse with water, then QA will take their tests. 
Curt Willis Charity Young

Erandy Garcilazo Ceja the covers from the adjacent areas has been removed.  

Do you know why?
Which covers are you talking about?  The only covers removed are the ones for the 2 belts we will finish to clean up today
A shipment has arrived. We should be good for at least a week.

Continuing with the buffer emptying test, the 3rd shift lead informed me that the line was started at 5:50 a.m. with the buffer tank at 98%. The line stopped at 8:36 a.m. with the buffer at 28% while running at 1.75 speed / 1,224 pieces per minute.
Approximately 70% of the buffer was used in 166 minutes, averaging about 25% per hour.
The buffer is currently at 32% after pending trim remelt chocolate was added back into the buffer tank once the line stopped.
After troubleshooting/tear down it was discovered to have a worn bearing which is the cause of the belt hopping/noise. The gearbox needed to be inspected, cleaned and it now has new grease as well.
The TL employees finished rotating the lines for lunch and started cleaning at 12:30pm

Can we put a catch tarp with hose to drain into a barrel to prevent it from falling into the floor  or people or equipment William  Lopez

It is condensation from dishwasher exhaust 
The exhaust pipe from dishwasher is next to hot air exhaust from boiler room because this morning is cooler than usual but our boiler room is still really hot that created condensation internally 




We are looking into solutions to prevent condensation.
Yes, will work on it now.

We are struggling to stage some items because we are short on available space. I was wondering if there was a plan for these hold Crumbl pallets that are on hold. We would like to clear it up, if possible to allow for more production item storage. Charity Young Miriah Manzanares
Danial and I are working on this now.  We will have an answer by tomorrow

Yes it was only a 10 minute pause

There was some miscommunication when an EE went to lunch. We were pausing for the pack end on the WDS, The EE on depositor 1 turned off sending the chocolate so in case the pause was longer we didn't over heat the chocolate. This was not communicated to the EE sending them to lunch so when we started back up the chocolate wasn't ready inside the depositor and it took a minute for the EE to notice. We have about 270 molds inside with no product which will be about 11-12 min of not running.
